commit.message:
feat: backend analysis pipeline + purchase/paywall system

Stage 10: Add STT (Deepgram), audio metrics extraction, LLM coaching
(OpenAI), analysis job orchestrator, and implement session/analysis
routes with Prisma. Mock fallbacks when API keys are missing.

Stage 12: Add PaywallScreen with monthly/lifetime plans, usePurchase
context, usePaywallGate hook. Gate days 3+, Script/Impromptu/Roleplay,
and mini-games behind paywall. Freestyle and days 1-2 remain free.

Consolidate unit toggle to one per section and fix reset bug

- Land/building section: single ㎡/坪 toggle in header controls all
  fields (area, land price, building price) at once
- Rent section: keeps its own single toggle
- Reset now rebuilds only the form while restoring DATA values via
  restoreFormValues(), so other sections' inputs are preserved

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add explicit initial opacity to card face motion.div elements

Without initial prop, Framer Motion can start elements at opacity:0 on mount
and delay showing them (due to delay:1.0 in transition). Explicitly setting
initial={{opacity:1}} for back face and initial={{opacity:0}} for front face
ensures correct render on mount with no animation delay.

Design System Phase 1: CSS tokens, Geist font, Tailwind rewire

Foundation layer for the design system overhaul:

- globals.css: Full CSS custom properties for colors (RGB channels),
  typography scale (5 levels), border radius, and dark-optimized shadows
- tailwind.config.ts: Colors rewired to rgb(var(...)) for opacity modifier
  support, added semantic fontSize/borderRadius/boxShadow/surface tokens
- layout.tsx: Load Geist Sans variable font via geist package

All existing Tailwind classes (bg-brand-*, text-sm, rounded-lg, etc.)
continue to work unchanged. New tokens (text-display, rounded-card,
bg-surface-raised, shadow-glow) are additive for Phase 2 migration.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: context-aware completion provider for .strm files (#27)

## Summary

- Context-aware completions scoped to what is grammatically valid at the
cursor position
- `in`/`out` snippet variants auto-pop on space after keyword (no
Ctrl+Space needed)
- After `in source -> queue` or `out queue -> dest`, space triggers
options scoped by source/dest type (CSV/S3/DB)
- `node name(inputs)(outputs)` space trigger offers all node-level
options (`lang=`, `exec_when=`, `where=`, `getfact=`, `setfact=`,
`getsig=`, `setsig=`, `getdyn=`, `setdyn=`)
- Model continuation (`model` line or indented line below it): suggests
`type=`, `label=`, `exceptq=`, `scenario=`, `unittest=`, `modelname=`;
excludes options already defined on the same line or preceding
continuation lines
- Top-level keywords cleaned up: only proper statements (`name`, `type`,
`label`, `descr`, `in`, `out`, `node`, `model`, `subprocess`); removed
ANTLR token dump and line-option keywords (`lang`, `db`, `mode`)
- Fixed `model` snippet: grammar has no node name — `model
(input1)(output1)` not `model nodeName(...)`
- `createCompletionProvider(csvCache)` extracted as exported factory for
unit testing
- 72 unit tests added covering all contexts and scoping rules

## Test plan

### Top-level (empty line)
- [x] Ctrl+Space on empty line shows: `name`, `type`, `label`, `descr`,
`in`, `out`, `node`, `model`, `subprocess`
- [x] Does NOT show: `lang`, `db`, `mode`, `exceptq`, or ANTLR document
tokens (node/queue names)

### `in` snippets
- [x] Type `in ` (space) → `in (CSV)`, `in (DB)`, `in (S3)` auto-pop
without Ctrl+Space
- [x] `in (CSV)` snippet inserts `file.csv -> input_queue` (no `in `
prefix duplication)

### `in` line options (after `->`)
- [x] `in input.csv -> raw_input ` (space) → shows `where=`, `name=` —
NOT `db=`, NOT `compress`
- [x] `in public.transactions -> db_input ` → shows `db=`, `where=`,
`name=`
- [x] `in s3://bucket/path -> s3_input ` → shows nothing (S3 inputs have
no options)
- [x] Already-used options are excluded (e.g. `where=` already on line →
not re-suggested)

### `out` snippets
- [x] Type `out ` (space) → `out (CSV)`, `out (DB)`, `out (S3)` auto-pop
without Ctrl+Space
- [x] `out (CSV)` snippet inserts `output_queue -> output.csv` (no `out
` prefix duplication)

### `out` line options (after `->`)
- [x] `out queue -> output.csv ` → shows `compress`, `exec_when=` — NOT
`db=`, NOT `mode=`
- [x] `out queue -> s3://bucket/path ` → shows `mode=`, `compress`,
`exec_when=` — NOT `db=`
- [x] `out queue -> analytics.results ` → shows `db=`, `compress`,
`exec_when=` — NOT `mode=`
- [x] Already-used flags excluded (e.g. `compress` already on line → not
re-suggested)

### `node` line options
- [x] `node filternode(raw_input)(filtered) ` (space after closing
paren) → auto-pops `lang=`, `exec_when=`, `where=`, `getfact=`,
`setfact=`, `getsig=`, `setsig=`, `getdyn=`, `setdyn=`
- [x] Options already on the line are excluded (e.g. `lang=go ` →
`lang=` not re-suggested)

### `model` snippet and continuation
- [x] Selecting `model` snippet inserts `model (input1)(output1)` — no
node name
- [x] After `model (enriched)(scored)`, Enter + indent, space or
Ctrl+Space shows: `type=`, `label=`, `exceptq=`, `scenario=`,
`unittest=`, `modelname=`
- [x] Options defined on previous continuation lines are excluded
- [x] Options defined on the model line itself (same line as cursor) are
excluded
- [x] When all 6 options are defined, suggestion list is empty

### No duplicate trigger
- [x] Accept `in (CSV)` snippet, tab through, press space after queue
name → shows `where=`/`name=` only (not `in (CSV)` snippets again)
- [x] Same for `out` — no re-trigger of out snippets after space on a
completed line

### CI
- [x] `npm test` passes (72 tests)
- [x] `npm run compile` passes
- [x] `npm run lint` passes

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
Beat 1: Foundation — cost tracking, backend-driven UI, portable sandbox, smoke tests

Four-AI council (Claude, Grok, GPT 5.4, Copilot DR) audited the codebase and
agreed on foundational gaps. This closes them all:

- Live cost ticker: per-provider token tracking piped through SSE to frontend
  with color-coded display and click-to-reset
- Backend-driven models: /api/models serves model list with pricing, frontend
  renders dynamically — no more hardcoded HTML drift
- Platform portability: sandbox default auto-detects repo root instead of
  hardcoded B:/Grok path
- Context engine tracked: context_engine.py was wired into runtime but
  untracked in git — now committed
- Config overhaul: EXECUTOR_MODELS expanded with pricing data, cost limits
  configurable via env vars
- New endpoints: /api/models, /api/config, /api/cost, /api/cost/reset,
  /api/memory, /api/memory/clear
- 29 smoke tests covering routes, plan parser, sandbox enforcement, lazy tool
  discovery, context engine, config validation, provider detection
- README rewritten to reflect actual project scope
- requirements.txt created (was missing entirely)

commit.message:
feat(engine): add DragDropSystem and extract solitaire constants

Introduce DragDropSystem (priority 50) into the engine with Draggable
and DropTarget data components, emitting dragdrop:before and
dragdrop:dropped events so game systems only need to supply rules.

SolitaireSystem refactored to subscribe to those events — mouse
hit-testing, drag movement, and snap-back are removed (~130 lines).
Shared layout/game-data constants extracted to solitaire/constants.ts
to eliminate duplication between index.ts and SolitaireSystem.ts.
A setDraggable() helper collapses four repeated draggable-toggle blocks.

Refactor GvG Teams: party-based data model, compact layout, Firestore fix

- Refactor from flat memberIds to independent parties arrays (3 parties per team)
- Each party is an independent drop target with positional reordering
- Store parties as Firestore map to avoid nested array limitation
- Backward-compatible loading for old memberIds and array formats
- Compact multi-column grid layout with responsive team cards
- Fix delete button cut off in narrow columns
- Add dashed separators between members
- Fix crown emoji height on Windows
- Remove old teams.html and update nav links

feat: generate color backgrounds for posts without images

Use MD5 hash of post URL to derive a hex color, then use The Color API
to provide a unique color image as the post list background and
OpenGraph meta image for posts without their own image.

Closes [Make the image background for post-list-item a hex-code from the hash of the post URL](https://github.com/joshbeckman/notes/issues/448)

Add click-to-expand for truncated content and preserve input on reconnect (#564)

Add click-to-expand for all truncated content and preserve input on reconnect

- New ExpandableText component for character-based truncation with toggle
- New ExpandableLines component for line-based truncation (Write tool)
- Replace hard truncation at 5 sites: tool results (500 chars), structured
  blocks (500 chars), generic tool args (40 chars), WebFetch prompts (100
  chars), and Write tool previews (20 lines)

  re-renders to prevent input loss

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

lean4: sorry-free formalization — program algebra + all proofs filled

Add uniqueNames hypothesis where needed, 7 private helper lemmas
(findNode_unique, executed_ne_frontier_name, etc.). Zero sorry remain.

ProgramAlgebra.lean: New module formalizing the algebra of Cell programs.
Program equivalence (observational), GraphOp independence, eval-one
traces, distillation as program transformation, bootstrap sequences,
frontier operations (describe/advance/transform). Non-vacuity witnesses
for hello-world bootstrap. Formal claim connecting algebra to syntax
design requirements.

* docs: make Terraform the default, deprecate CDK

  CDK moved to deprecated section

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(rale): support un-pinned USLs with authorizer-side manifest pinning

Clients can now send a USL without a manifest hash
(/<registry>/<author>/<pkg>/<key>) and the RALE authorizer resolves
the latest hash from a `pkg:{registry}/{package_name}` pointer item in
the manifest-cache table before issuing the TAJ.  The RALE router
similarly accepts un-pinned paths and takes the hash from the TAJ
rather than the URL.

Infrastructure changes:
- Add MANIFEST_CACHE_TABLE env var to rale_authorizer Lambda
- Add dynamodb:GetItem on manifest_cache to rale_authorizer IAM policy

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

tf-outputs.json written directly from `terraform output -json`. All
consumers (integration helpers, compiler, load_policies, build-envoy-image)
now reference snake_case Terraform output keys directly.

commit.message:
fix: switch codex upload to presigned URLs for iPhone/Vercel compat

Vercel serverless functions have a ~4.5MB body limit, which rejects
virtually any video upload. Now uses a two-step flow: 1) get presigned
R2 URL from server, 2) PUT video directly to R2 from client with XHR
progress. Falls back to FormData upload for local dev without R2.
Lenient MIME type checking for iPhone video formats.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: session messages load on card click navigation (#500)

* fix: session messages load on card click navigation

Previously, clicking a session card would navigate to the session detail
page but messages wouldn't load until a page refresh. This was caused by
a timing issue where useSessionMessages initialized with empty messages
while the session data was still loading.

The fix adds an isLoading parameter to useSessionMessages that prevents
message initialization until data loading completes. Uses a reducer
pattern to properly handle session changes and initial data sync.

- Add isLoading prop to UseSessionMessagesOptions interface
- Refactor from useState to useReducer for proper state management
- Pass isLoading from session page to useSessionMessages hook
- Add comprehensive unit tests for the loading behavior
- Add E2E tests for session navigation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update fleece issues

* fix: E2E test back button selector to target main content area

The session navigation E2E test was failing because it found the first
link with an SVG icon, which matched sidebar navigation links instead
of the back button in the session header. Fixed by scoping the selector
to look within the main content area only.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
